gate_type: wp_nonce admin taxonomy-edit-screen + capability check edit_terms (manage_categories = Editor role)
reasoning: The exploitation path requires two gates that an unauthenticated or low-privilege attacker cannot breach. Gate 1: a WordPress nonce generated by wp_nonce_field() inside the taxonomy edit admin screen (action: 'auhfc_{taxonomy}_save_action'). This nonce is only rendered to users who are authenticated and hold sufficient privileges to load the WP admin taxonomy edit page. An unauthenticated attacker cannot obtain it. Gate 2: current_user_can($tax_obj->cap->edit_terms, $term_id) at line 124. For built-in taxonomies (category, post_tag), edit_terms maps to manage_categories, which requires Editor role or higher in a standard WordPress install. Subscribers and Contributors cannot access taxonomy edit screens at all. The finding is NOT default-exploitable by unauthenticated or low-privilege users. However, severity is kept at MEDIUM (not downgraded to LOW/INFORMATIONAL) because: (a) the capability gate sits below the WordPress-standard unfiltered_html gate that core uses to allow raw script storage; (b) Editor is a role that sites commonly grant to non-owner content managers who should not be able to inject JavaScript for all visitors; (c) the taint path is confirmed end-to-end and the XSS executes on all taxonomy archive pages. The gate_breachable flag is false because the prerequisite is an authenticated Editor session plus a valid nonce from that session, which is not obtainable by an unauthenticated or Subscriber/Contributor-level attacker. A site granting Editor-or-above to untrusted users is in-scope for exploitation.
